pipeline.description: 9-step verification pipeline: validate envelope integrity, verify identity, enforce scope, burn nonce, audit.

steps.name: envelope_exists
steps.description: Check that a sealed envelope JSON file exists in gate/incoming/ and can be parsed. Verify all required fields are present: envelope_id, payload, payload_hash, nonce, timestamp, user_fingerprint, machine_fingerprint, scope.
steps.command: python -c "from boundaries.transition_gate import TransitionEnvelope; e = TransitionEnvelope.from_file('gate/incoming/envelope_*.json'); print('OK')"
steps.expected_output: Envelope loaded with all required fields present.

steps.name: payload_integrity
steps.description: Recompute SHA-256 hash of the payload using canonical JSON serialization (sorted keys, no extra whitespace). Compare against envelope.payload_hash using timing-safe comparison.

steps.name: fingerprint_match
steps.description: Recompute user fingerprint using the shared secret (from Windows Credential Manager) and the machine_fingerprint embedded in the envelope. Compare against envelope.user_fingerprint using hmac.compare_digest (timing-safe).

steps.name: nonce_valid
steps.description: Check that the nonce exists in the shared nonce registry, has not been burned, and has not expired. If valid, mark as pending-burn (actual burn happens after all checks pass).
steps.expected_output: Nonce is valid, unburned, and within max_age_seconds.

steps.name: timestamp_fresh
steps.description: Verify that the envelope's timestamp is within max_age_seconds (default 600s / 10 minutes) of current time. Reject stale envelopes to prevent delayed replay.

steps.name: tests_verified
steps.description: Verify that the source reports tests_passed == true in the envelope. Optionally verify lint_passed == true if require_lint is configured. These reflect the source's quality gate execution, not a local re-run.

steps.name: scope_present
steps.description: Verify that the envelope contains a non-empty scope declaration with at least one permission. The scope must use only known_permissions from the contract.
steps.expected_output: Scope declaration present with recognized permissions.

steps.name: deploy_within_scope
steps.description: Verify that the requested action (e.g. deploy, run_tests, start_server) is listed in the envelope's scope.permissions. Reject any action not explicitly granted. Unknown permissions are always rejected.
steps.expected_output: Requested action is within the declared scope permissions.

steps.name: audit_log
steps.description: Burn the nonce (mark as used in nonce registry, persist to disk). Append a verification result entry to gate/audit.ndjson with envelope_id, timestamp, status, reason, step results, and duration. Feed the event to Overwatch if integration is enabled.
steps.expected_output: Nonce burned. Audit entry appended. Overwatch event emitted.

never_rules.rule: Never accept an envelope whose payload_hash does not match the recomputed SHA-256 of the payload. Any mismatch means the payload was tampered with after sealing.
Never accept an envelope whose user_fingerprint does not match the recomputed HMAC-SHA256. A mismatch means the secret or machine identity differs from the sealer.
Never accept a replayed nonce. Once a nonce is burned, any subsequent envelope presenting the same nonce must be immediately rejected.
Never accept an envelope older than max_age_seconds. Stale envelopes are a replay risk even if the nonce hasn't been burned (clock-based defense in depth).
Never execute an action outside the envelope's declared scope permissions. If the scope says read_only, do not deploy, start servers, or write results.
Never store or log the user_secret. The shared secret is used only transiently for HMAC recomputation and must not persist in logs, audit trails, or config files.
Never skip the audit_log step, even on rejection. Every verification attempt (pass or fail) must produce an append-only NDJSON audit entry for forensic review.
Never use string equality (==) for fingerprint or hash comparison. Always use hmac.compare_digest or equivalent timing-safe comparison to prevent timing side-channel attacks.
Never accept an envelope with tests_passed=false when require_tests is enabled. The quality gate is non-negotiable.
Never process an envelope from a source_partition that does not match the expected source in the contract. Only E:\ is a trusted source.
Never allow fail-open behavior. If any verification step encounters an unexpected error, the entire envelope must be rejected. Ambiguity is treated as hostile.
Never modify the envelope payload before or during verification. The verification pipeline operates on an immutable snapshot; any mutation invalidates the integrity proof.

lots-server.description: Light of the Seven deployment target

experiments.description: Experimental workspace (no deploy or server permissions)

incoming.description: Sealed envelopes land here for verification

results.description: Verification results and processed envelope metadata

audit.ndjson.description: Append-only NDJSON audit trail for all verification events

.nonce_registry.json.description: Persistent nonce registry (burned nonces tracked here)

archive.description: Archived old source-like folders from pre-gate era (read-only, no deploy)

migration_checklist.task: Store shared secret in Windows Credential Manager: cmdkey /add:TransitionGate /user:USER /pass:<secret>
Archive existing source-like folders (Afloat/, GRID-main/, etc.) to archive/ if not needed as deploy targets
Create MANIFEST.md at C:\Users\USER\cascadeprojects\ with header row documenting deployed artifacts
